event_name: shai_hulululud npm Package Uses Prompt Injection and Token Flooding to Disrupt AI Malware Scanners
confidence_reason: Public evidence confirms the package identity, publication timestamp, and anti-analysis framing. The current evidence does not prove downstream credential theft or a second-stage payload.

artifact_analysis.execution_trigger: package inspection or JavaScript execution of index.js after install
payload_behavior: Large JavaScript file dominated by repeated comment lines intended to flood or disrupt AI-assisted review
Obfuscated JavaScript appended near the end of the file
Contains eval() usage in the retrieved artifact

open_questions: Public reporting does not prove whether the package contains a functional second-stage payload beyond anti-analysis and obfuscation.
No public source reviewed here confirms package removal or additional related publisher artifacts.
Technique overlap with Mini Shai-Hulud, Miasma, and Hades is suggestive, but shared operator identity is not proven.

defender_takeaways.detection: Search dependency records, package caches, and artifact stores for the exact package name, version, tarball URL, and tarball hashes.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Treat repositories or scanners that ingested the package as potentially disrupted and verify that human review, not only AI automation, assessed the artifact.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Remove the package from any test mirrors or caches, block the exact package/version, and document whether review tooling crashed or skipped analysis.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Require deterministic package quarantine and static artifact inspection for unusually large single-file packages with heavy repetition or obfuscation.

containment_complete: All internal mirrors, caches, and sandboxes block or quarantine shai_hulululud@1.0.48596.
eradication_complete: The package no longer appears in package caches, lockfiles, or review queues.
recovery_complete: Human review confirms no dependent automation silently skipped package analysis because of context flooding or safety-trigger handling.
closure_required: Evidence shows the package was either never ingested or was removed from every affected scanning and repository environment.
